sms_pump_no_ratelimit.description: SMS-sending endpoint (OTP, magic-link, password-reset, 2FA enroll) has no per-recipient or per-IP rate limit -- attacker forces target to incur per-SMS provider cost (Twilio $0.0075-$0.075 each, $1k-$10k/day burn)

probes.description: Re-issue SMS-send request 5-10 times in rapid succession -- response 200 each time with no 429/captcha-challenge = no rate limit. Confirm with concurrent_requests(concurrency=10).

email_pump_no_ratelimit.description: Email-sending endpoint (password reset, magic link, invite, verification) accepts unlimited requests -- target burns transactional-email quota (SendGrid $0.0008/email at 100k/day = $80/day attack cost to victim, but reputation damage + bounce-rate spike disables sending platform-wide)

probes.description: Repeatedly submit password-reset or invite for the same email -- 200 each time = email pump. Critical when target user is real (mailbox-flooding attack).

otp_brute_no_lockout.description: OTP / 2FA verify endpoint has no attempt counter -- 6-digit numeric = 1M tries, parallel 100rps = 167 min to exhaust

probes.description: Submit 20 wrong OTPs against the same session -- if 20th still returns 'invalid' instead of 'too many attempts' / lockout, the entire code space is reachable via concurrent_requests

expensive_query_no_limit.description: Listing / search endpoint accepts unbounded `limit` / `per_page` / `count` causing DB scan + serialization of 100k+ rows (CPU/memory exhaustion, response-size amplification)

probes.description: Request 100k records -- timing > baseline * 50 OR response length > baseline * 1000 = no upper bound enforced
INT_MAX limit -- if server attempts the query at all (200 with long delay), it has no upper-bound clamp

graphql_alias_DoS.description: GraphQL query duplicates the same expensive field via aliases (no depth limit, no query-cost analysis) -- single request burns N× CPU

probes.payload: {"query":"{a0:users{id} a1:users{id} a2:users{id} a3:users{id} a4:users{id} a5:users{id} a6:users{id} a7:users{id} a8:users{id} a9:users{id}}"}
probes.description: 10× alias-duplicated query -- if executed (200 with N× length) the server has no alias/cost limit

file_upload_no_size_limit.description: Upload endpoint accepts arbitrarily large body -- attacker fills disk / triggers OOM. Test by streaming size via Content-Length header probe.

probes.description: Send upload with Content-Length: 10737418240 (10 GiB) but small actual body -- 200/201 = no streaming size check, 413 expected on healthy server

biometric_or_paid_provider_call.description: Endpoint triggers paid backend service per call (biometric validation, KYC check, payment-card verification, geocoding API) without per-user/per-IP rate limit -- target pays vendor cost per attacker request

probes.description: Re-issue paid-provider request 5× rapidly -- 200 each = unmetered fan-out to vendor. Confirm via vendor-specific cost in target docs.

zip_bomb_decompression.description: Upload endpoint accepts compressed input (zip, gzip, brotli) and decompresses server-side without size cap -- 10MB zip bomb expands to 10GB

probes.description: Upload nested zip with high compression ratio (1:1000+) and Content-Encoding: gzip -- 500/OOM/timeout = server decompresses without limit
